# Per-group deviation: windowcomp avg by group, then subtract

Flag outliers without a stddev divisor (XQL has no window stddev): `windowcomp avg(<metric>) by <group> as grp_avg` attaches each row's GROUP mean as a per-row column, then `alter dev = subtract(<metric>, grp_avg)` is how far that row sits above/below its peers. Verified: ivan's 250000 vs sales-dept avg 133333 → dev +116666. The biggest positive deviations are the anomalies. IR use: per-process upload volume vs the per-host average exposes the one process exfiltrating far more than its neighbors.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| comp sum(action_total_upload) as up by agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name
| windowcomp avg(up) by agent_hostname as host_avg_up
| alter up_excess = subtract(up, host_avg_up)
| sort desc up_excess
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
